hints.apud: 'apud' is a preposition meaning 'at the house of / in the presence of'
hints.Laecam: 'Laecam' is a proper noun — the name Laeca in accusative
hints.nocte: 'nocte' is a noun in the ablative case meaning 'at night'

hints.to flee: 'to flee' is 'fugere' — 'fuisti' comes from 'esse' (to be), perfect tense
hints.to become: 'to become' is 'fieri' — 'fuisti' is the perfect of 'esse'

hints.Present indicative: Present would be 'es' — 'fuisti' with the perfect stem fu- is perfect tense

hints.fio, fieri, factus sum: These are the parts of 'fieri' (to become/be made)
hints.fugio, fugere, fugi, fugitum: These are the parts of 'fugere' (to flee)
hints.fundo, fundere, fudi, fusum: These are the parts of 'fundere' (to pour)

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'Laeca' — 'Laecam' with -am ending is accusative after 'apud'
hints.Dative: Dative would be 'Laecae' — 'Laecam' is accusative after 'apud'
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'Laeca' — 'apud' governs the accusative

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'nox' — 'nocte' with -e ending is ablative of time
hints.Dative: Dative would be 'nocti' — 'nocte' is ablative of time when

hints.1st: 1st declension ablative ends in -a — 'nox, noctis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension ablative ends in -o — 'nox, noctis' is 3rd declension
hints.5th: 5th declension ablative ends in -e as well, but 'nox, noctis' with gen. in -is is 3rd declension

steps.prompt: What type of ablative is 'illa nocte'?

hints.Ablative of means: Ablative of means answers 'by what instrument' — 'nocte' answers 'when'
hints.Ablative absolute: Ablative absolute requires a participle + noun — 'illa nocte' is simply time when
hints.Ablative of manner: Ablative of manner describes how — 'illa nocte' describes when

steps.prompt: Why does Cicero name the specific host and night?
steps.target: To prove he has detailed intelligence about the conspiracy
choices: He is making a general accusation
To prove he has detailed intelligence about the conspiracy
He is guessing at the details

hints.He is making a general accusation: The specific details (Laeca, that night) show Cicero knows exactly what happened
hints.He is guessing at the details: The precision of 'apud Laecam illa nocte' demonstrates confirmed intelligence, not guesswork
hints.To praise Laeca's hospitality: Laeca hosted a secret meeting of conspirators — Cicero names him to implicate him

translation: You were at Laeca's house on that night.
